$comment: Tier-2 DB-state fixture spec for the `users` repo (Phase-2). One committed
file drives BOTH sides of the differential:
- the Rust port (quilltap-harness users tier-2 test).
What this repo banks: the plainest marshaling shape so far â€” one required
TEXT column (`username`) plus five NULLABLE TEXT columns (`email`, `name`,
BLOB. null/undefined -> SQL NULL; a string -> the string. The corpus pairs a
fully-populated row (every nullable set, a plausible passwordHash, an
emailVerified timestamp) against a minimal row (username only, all nullables
null), and exercises nullable->value sets via update.
SAFETY: testPepperBase64 is a THROWAWAY 32-byte key, the same one the
folders / tags / text_replacement_rules / image_profiles fixtures use. It is
NOT the real Friday pepper and is safe to commit.
DETERMINISM: every id and timestamp is pinned, so both sides produce
byte-identical post-op state and the canonical dump needs ZERO normalization.

ops.comment: New fully-populated user: every nullable column set, a plausible passwordHash, an emailVerified timestamp.
New minimal user: username only, all nullable columns null.
Update seed row 1 (fully-populated): rename and change email/image. createdAt preserved, updatedAt pinned.
Update seed row 2 (minimal): set email + name (nullable -> value). updatedAt pinned.
